violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. A container of pork carnitas in the large cooler in the kitchen was dated 5/29. A container of beef was dated 6/1. A container of queso in the small fridge was dated 5/26. These exceed the 10 days allowed.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. The wood Len ledge/shelf on the East side of the bar is bare wood. This is not a cleanable surface and needs to be painted or sealed.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. An open box of 32 oz deli containers were stored on the floor in the stockroom.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. A container of green sauce on the prep table temped at 69 degrees. This must be kept refrigerated.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
Cooking and reheating to the proper time and temperature (5 pts deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
